content:
# Fluid Loss

**Summary**: The reduction of the volume of the liquid phase of a slurry into the surrounding formation.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] measured fluid loss to evaluate the effectiveness of additives in maintaining slurry volume.

- [[2014-hashmat|Hashmat, 2014]] evaluated the effectiveness of cross-linked polymer gels for fluid-loss control.

- [[2017-baig|Baig, 2017]] measured fluid loss in various slurries.

- [[2023-ahmed-2|AHMED, 2023]] measured fluid loss to ensure the stability and integrity of the cement column.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] measured fluid loss to ensure proper volume control and gas migration prevention.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] Controlling fluid loss is critical to ensure enough cement reaches the target depth.

- [[2014-hashmat|Hashmat, 2014]] evaluated the effectiveness of cross-linked polymer gels for fluid-loss control.

- [[2017-baig|Baig, 2017]] measured fluid loss in various slurries.

- [[2023-ahmed-2|AHMED, 2023]] measured fluid loss to ensure the stability and integrity of the cement column.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] measured fluid loss to ensure proper volume control and gas migration prevention.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] Standardized fluid loss tests were conducted on the samples.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Addition of Saudi bentonite and Nano clay reduced fluid loss under HPHT conditions. ([[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015, p. 21]])
- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] Reduced by addition of bentonite and Nano clay.

- Not stated in source ([[2014-hashmat|Hashmat, 2014]])

- Target values for different operations range from <50 to 300 ml/30 min. ([[2017-baig|Baig, 2017, p. 58]])

- Barite nanoparticles reduced fluid loss by 50% compared to standard barite. ([[2023-ahmed-2|AHMED, 2023, p. 77]])

- The developed geopolymer had a fluid loss of 38 mL/30 min, meeting API standards for high-performance slurries. ([[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023, p. 67]])
